evals.prompt: Prepare a detailed engineer's estimate for a 20 km trunk road upgrade in Uganda (gravel to paved DBST). The BoQ (from Skill #51) shows: Bill 1 Preliminaries UGX 1.5B, Bill 2 Earthworks UGX 3.0B, Bill 3 Pavement UGX 5.5B, Bill 4 Drainage UGX 2.0B, Bill 5 Structures UGX 0, Bill 6 Miscellaneous UGX 0.8B, Bill 7 Dayworks UGX 0.7B. Contract duration is 2 years. Use detailed design contingency level. All rates are PLACEHOLDER.
evals.expected_output: Estimate showing total direct cost UGX 13.5B, physical contingency at 8-12% (detailed design), price contingency for 2-year contract, VAT at 18%, total estimate with all additions. Cost per km calculated and compared to Uganda benchmarks. PLACEHOLDER disclaimer prominent.

assertions.text: BoQ quantities referenced from Skill #51 output with Bill subtotals
Unit rates referenced from Skill #52/#53 (even if PLACEHOLDER)
Physical contingency at detailed design level (8-12%, typically 10%)
Price contingency applied for multi-year contract with inflation rate stated
VAT at 18% included and total clearly marked PLACEHOLDER

evals.prompt: Prepare a preliminary cost estimate for a 50 km national road improvement in Uganda, funded by the World Bank (IDA credit). Project is at preliminary design stage. BoQ not yet detailed -- use approximate quantities. The estimate must show both UGX and USD equivalents in World Bank cost table format. Contract duration is 3 years. Include the foreign/local currency split.
evals.expected_output: Preliminary estimate with higher contingency (15-20%), USD conversion shown alongside UGX, World Bank cost table format referenced, 3-year price contingency with expenditure profile, foreign/local currency split (approximately 65/35). PLACEHOLDER disclaimer.

assertions.text: Higher physical contingency for preliminary stage (15-20%)
USD conversion shown with exchange rate stated and flagged as illustrative
World Bank cost table format referenced or approximated
Price contingency for 3-year contract with expenditure profile (e.g., 25/45/30)
Foreign/local currency split indicated for donor-funded context

evals.prompt: Prepare a cost estimate for emergency rehabilitation of a 10 km road section in Northern Uganda damaged by flooding. No detailed design available -- only a rapid damage assessment. Works include: debris clearing, temporary drainage restoration, emergency surface patching, and culvert replacement (3 washed-out culverts). The works are urgent and must start within 2 weeks.
evals.expected_output: Estimate with high contingency (20-25%) reflecting lack of detailed design. Simplified BoQ (not all 7 Bills needed). Emergency/urgency premium noted (e.g., mobilisation cost higher, limited competitive pricing). Rates flagged as approximate and PLACEHOLDER. Northern Uganda regional cost adjustment mentioned.

assertions.text: High contingency (20-25%) appropriate for emergency works without detailed design
Simplified BoQ structure appropriate for emergency scope
Emergency/urgency pricing adjustment noted (higher mobilisation, limited competition)
All rates flagged as approximate and PLACEHOLDER

evals.prompt: Prepare a preliminary engineer's estimate for a 30km Class B road rehabilitation project in North Eastern Kenya. The project includes base repair, AC overlay, drainage improvements, and road furniture. The project is funded by the African Development Bank. Use WSCM Part 3 BoQ format.
evals.expected_output: Estimate using WSCM Part 3 series structure (Series 1000-9000). North Eastern Kenya regional cost factor (1.25-1.45) applied to base rates. Both KES and USD amounts shown (donor-funded). PPRA threshold implications noted. Physical contingency at 15-20% for preliminary design stage. All rates flagged as PLACEHOLDER. VAT at 16% (or exempt per donor agreement -- flag for verification).

assertions.text: References WSCM Part 3 format with series numbering (Series 1000-9000)
Applies regional cost factor for North Eastern Kenya (1.25-1.45 range or similar remote area adjustment)
All rates and cost figures flagged as PLACEHOLDER
PPRA threshold implications mentioned (procurement method determination based on estimate value)
Dual currency (KES and USD) presentation for donor-funded context
